top_review:
One of the best spots in Purwokerto for coffee, pastries, and light meals. We came as a big family group and ordered a wide variety of items from their menu.

The staff were quick, responsive, and easy to communicate with. All orders were taken promptly and everything arrived on time without any hassle.

I had a cold brew and their Arastreat chicken meal. The chicken was delicious, and the crispy kailan added a nice texture that complemented the dish well.

The cold brew was served thoughtfully, with water and cookies on the side. Their coffee roast was excellent, and I even grabbed an americano to go afterwards.

This café is also a great option for visitors who want to work from a café, as each table comes with power outlets and a stable Wi-Fi connection.

Sunday: 10:00 AM – 12:00 AM
top_review:
I recently visited AT:NINE, hoping for a pleasant dining experience, but unfortunately, it left much to be desired.

From the start, the service was disappointing. The order taker seemed rude and impatient, constantly asking, "Anything else?" in a rushed tone. It felt like they wanted us to place our order as quickly as possible, leaving no room for us to make decisions at a comfortable pace. To make things worse, there were no waiters to deliver the drinks to the table, which felt like a letdown for the price we were paying.

Given the service and overall experience, the food did not feel worth the price. While the menu might look appealing, the actual value of the meal fell short of expectations.

The place itself is nice, with modern décor and a good layout. However, the air conditioning was poorly managed. Even though it was supposedly set to 16°C, it felt closer to 30°C inside, making the environment uncomfortable.

Sunday: 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM
top_review:
I visited this place after a recommendation, and I have to say the setting is very pleasant—relaxing, comfortable, and with a nice atmosphere.

I ordered an iced cappuccino, which was enjoyable and had a good coffee flavor. Unfortunately, the grilled chicken sandwich was a big letdown. The menu described it as a panini, but what I received was more like a grilled flatbread. After the first bite, it became even more disappointing—completely bland, with no salt, no pepper, no sauce, and nothing to bring out the taste of the chicken or tomatoes. If this is meant to be a panini, it really missed the mark.

Thankfully, the desserts were much better. The doughnuts had generous sweet and savory fillings, the almond croissant was rich in flavor with plenty of almond filling, and the crombolini was also packed with flavor and cream. These items clearly showed more attention to taste and quality.

Overall, this café has a lot of potential. The coffee and pastries are worth coming back for, but the savory food really needs improvement. With a bit more care in preparation and seasoning, this place could be so much better.

top_review: Waitress services and food tastes aside, since everyone has talked about it on the other comments ((Not that bad actually, in my experiences)). The cafe is quite good. In addition to its very affordable prices, especially with the student discount, the cafe has a cool atmosphere that makes you feel at ease. I took a seat outside, where the view of Gunung Slamet was a feast for the eyes. And one thing that amazed me was the toilet! This is something that seems like it would be the last consideration when choosing a cafe, but it's very clear that this cafe has its own value. The restrooms are clean and fully stocked with amenities (the women's restroom even provides sanitary pads with black plastic bags). I really appreciate your consideration for us 😍

There weren't many or a variety of options on the menu. there were too many rice bowls, and the fried noodles only had vegetables instead of chicken or eggs. The place is family-friendly, with a playground for kids, a musholla, and even a self-service coffee corner, which is a nice extra. The atmosphere is relaxed and suitable for dining with family. top_review:
Hi, I am Zico.
I am going to review a café namely My Story Resto.

My Story Resto is located in Purwokerto.

It’s a modern and cozy café that serves various types of food and beverages.

This café is well known for its tasty food, friendly service, and affordable price.

The interior wall of this café is well and beautifully decorated with plants, warm lights, and wooden furniture.

My favorite spot is the corner seat near the window.

I recently dined there as my friend advised me to try this place.

For the appetizer, I ordered French fries.
It’s crispy and salty in a good way. It was delicious!

For the main course, I ordered grilled chicken with rice.
It’s juicy and flavorful. It was so good!

The ambience is truly relaxing and perfect for hanging out with friends.

Overall, I would really recommend My Story resto to anyone

top_review:
Dapur Ora Umum has a concept that feels similar to Warung Upnormal, with a casual and modern warung-style setup. They provide a nobar area, separate smoking and non-smoking rooms, which makes it comfortable for different preferences. I expected the place to be very crowded, but it turned out quite calm when I visited.

They serve many meal options, so there’s plenty to choose from. I tried the nasi goreng hijau and it was good, flavorful, and enjoyable, while the other menu items I tried were more on the moderate side. Overall, it’s a comfortable place to eat, hang out, or watch together with friends, with a relaxed atmosphere and decent food choices.

Cafe Patawi is truly a hidden gem. The coffee is excellent—rich, well-balanced, and clearly made with care. Their cookies are just as impressive: fresh, delicious, and the perfect match for a cup of coffee.
The cozy atmosphere makes it a great place to relax, and it’s definitely one of those spots you’ll want to come back to again and again. If you’re looking for good coffee and tasty treats away from the crowds, Cafe Patawi is a must-visit.
